text:
Once upon an autumn dusk, little Bunny hoppered through the leaf-studded woods. On his tiny nose twitched an idea: the grand oak tree’s golden acorns. With hoppy gusto, he clambered up the sturdy bark.

Little did he know, wickedowl owl eyepeek.

The owl fluttered clumsily.

“Who?”

“Oh nothing... just pondering oak acorns.”

The owl tilted its head.

“That’s just silly.”

But oh how little owl knew that meddling owls love acorns?

With an owl feather tangled in his hair, little Bunny swung anxiously.

“Ow?” Owowl owl.

“It’s nothing… Ow?”

A branch swept.

With a soft THWOOFOWOFOW owl sound, the branch shattered.

Poor bunny—

“OWOWOWOOOOF?”

—

Attatched!

A big old owl clamped him tight.

“Oh dear.”

Owl guffawed.

“Oh?”

“Just...” Owliceowlowl.
